title: Make a model incremental only when it stays correct
description: Adopt incremental materialization with a late-arriving window and idempotent merge so it matches a full refresh.
body: Use when a full refresh of a large fact table is too slow or costly. Do not switch to incremental without handling late-arriving and updated rows — a naive append misses backdated data and drifts from a full refresh. Actions: define the incremental predicate with a lookback window covering late arrivals, use a merge/upsert on a stable key for idempotency, and periodically reconcile against a full refresh. Validation signals include an incremental build matching a full refresh on the same input and a reconciliation job that detects drift. Risk boundary: a drifting incremental model silently reports wrong totals; if reconciliation finds a gap, treat the model as untrustworthy and full-refresh while widening the window.
